triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to meet the LTV requirement.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy Section 4.1 states that the maximum LTV for Tier A is 110%. The calculated LTV is 91.11%, which is within the acceptable range.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio is within the acceptable range for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: No immediate action required as the DTI is compliant.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy Section 3.1 states that the maximum DTI for Tier A is 45%. The calculated DTI is 31.25%, which is compliant.

content.reasoning: The proposal was analyzed against the underwriting policy. The applicant's credit score places them in Tier A, allowing for a maximum LTV of 110% and a DTI of 45%. The calculated LTV is 91.11%, and the DTI is 31.25%, both within acceptable limits. No discrepancies or violations were found in the provided data.
